title: Jim Ferlo becomes the Pennsylvania Senate's first openly gay member
description: State Senator Jim Ferlo came out publicly, making him the first openly gay legislator to serve in the Pennsylvania Senate.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maura Healey elected first openly gay state attorney general in the US
description: Voters in Massachusetts elected Maura Healey as attorney general, the first openly gay person to win a state attorney general's office anywhere in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Aditi Hardikar named White House LGBT liaison
description: Aditi Hardikar took over as the White House's permanent liaison to the LGBT community, the first woman of color to hold the post. She followed interim liaison Monique Dorsainvil after Gautam Raghavan stepped down.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Wisconsin gets its first openly transgender elected official
description: Gypsy Vered Meltzer won a seat on the Appleton City Council, becoming the first openly transgender person elected to public office in Wisconsin.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Costa Rica flies the pride flag at the presidential palace
description: Costa Rica raised the rainbow flag over its presidential palace. The International Gay and Lesbian Human Rights Commission described it as the first known instance of the pride flag flying at a head of state's offices in the Americas.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cyprus holds its first pride parade
description: Several thousand people took part in the first pride parade ever held in Cyprus.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hong Kong hosts its first international LGBTI rights symposium
description: Hong Kong convened its first international symposium devoted to the rights of LGBTI people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First serving Chilean soldier comes out as gay
description: Marine Mauricio Ruiz announced he is gay, the first active member of Chile's armed forces to do so publicly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: California bans gay and trans panic defenses
description: California outlawed the use of gay panic and trans panic defenses in murder trials, the first US state to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: C1 Financial lists as first US public bank with an openly gay CEO
description: When Florida-based C1 Financial began trading in August 2014, its chief executive Trevor Burgess became the first openly gay CEO of a publicly listed US bank.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tim Cook comes out as gay
description: Apple chief executive Tim Cook publicly came out as gay, making him the first openly gay CEO of a Fortune 500 company.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: OutVets marches in Boston's Veterans Day parade
description: A contingent from OutVets took part in Boston's Veterans Day parade, the first LGBT organization ever to march in it.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Bisexual Health Awareness Month declared
description: The Boston-based Bisexual Resource Center declared March 2014 the first Bisexual Health Awareness Month, running a social media campaign on the physical and mental health disparities facing bisexual people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Queen Elizabeth II sends greetings to the London LGBT switchboard
description: Queen Elizabeth II sent congratulations to the London Lesbian and Gay Switchboard on its 40th anniversary, regarded as the first public message of support for the LGBT community from the British Crown.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ireland legalizes same-sex marriage by popular vote
description: Ireland approved same-sex marriage in a national referendum, the first country to do so by popular vote, with the change taking effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage becomes legal nationwide in the United States
description: The US Supreme Court's Obergefell v. Hodges ruling made same-sex marriage legal in every state. Earlier the same year it had already begun in Florida and in several tribal nations, including the Central Council of the Tlingit and Haida Tribes, the Oneida Nation of Wisconsin, and the Confederated Tribes of Siletz Indians.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Three Mexican states legalize same-sex marriage
description: Same-sex marriage was approved and took effect in the Mexican states of Chihuahua, Guerrero and Nayarit.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Luxembourg
description: Luxembourg's same-sex marriage law came into force, giving couples the right to marry and to adopt jointly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chile enacts civil unions
description: Chile passed a civil union law covering same-sex couples, and it came into effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ecuador expands civil union rights
description: Ecuador broadened its civil union framework for same-sex couples, with the expanded provisions taking effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cyprus passes a civil partnership law
description: The Cypriot parliament approved legislation creating civil partnerships open to same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Greece passes a civil partnership law
description: The Greek parliament approved legislation opening civil partnerships to same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Austria allows same-sex couples to adopt
description: Austria opened joint adoption to same-sex couples through legislation adopted in 2015.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ireland allows same-sex couples to adopt
description: Ireland adopted legislation permitting same-sex couples to adopt children.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mozambique decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Mozambique removed the criminalization of same-sex relations from its law, ending colonial-era provisions against homosexuality.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alberta protects gender identity and expression from discrimination
description: The Canadian province of Alberta added gender identity and gender expression to its anti-discrimination law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Madhu Kinnar becomes India's first openly transgender mayor
description: Madhu Kinnar won the mayoralty of the Raigarh Municipal Corporation in Chhattisgarh, the first openly transgender person elected mayor in India.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Leo Varadkar becomes Ireland's first openly gay government minister
description: Irish Health Minister Leo Varadkar came out as gay, the first serving member of an Irish government to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Wade MacLauchlan sworn in as Canada's first openly gay male premier
description: After winning the Prince Edward Island Liberal leadership on 21 February, Wade MacLauchlan was sworn in as provincial premier on 23 February — Canada's second out LGBT premier and its first out gay man in the office. His party's win in the 4 May election also made him the province's first out LGBT member of its Legislative Assembly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alberta elects its first openly LGBT legislators
description: Michael Connolly, Ricardo Miranda and Estefania Cortes-Vargas became Alberta's first three openly LGBT members of the Legislative Assembly. Cortes-Vargas later came out as genderqueer, becoming the first out trans person to serve in any Canadian legislature.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nepal's new constitution protects LGBT rights
description: Nepal adopted its first democratic constitution, the first in Asia to explicitly protect the rights of lesbian, gay, bisexual and transgender people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tamara Adrián elected to Venezuela's National Assembly
description: Tamara Adrián won a seat in Venezuela's National Assembly, the first openly transgender person elected to a national legislature anywhere in the Western Hemisphere.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Aisha Moodie-Mills takes over leadership of the Victory Fund
description: Aisha Moodie-Mills became president and CEO of the Victory Fund — the first woman, first Black woman, and first lesbian to head a leading national LGBT organization in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First transgender rights bills introduced in Pennsylvania
description: State Representative Mark B. Cohen introduced the first bills addressing transgender rights in Pennsylvania's legislative history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kate Brown becomes the first openly bisexual US governor
description: Kate Brown took office as governor of Oregon, the first openly bisexual person to serve as a state governor in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Salt Lake City elects its first openly gay mayor
description: Jackie Biskupski won election as mayor of Salt Lake City, the first openly gay person to hold the office.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First out lesbian elected to a legislative body in Tennessee
description: Nancy VanReece won the Metro Council District 8 seat in Nashville, becoming the first out lesbian elected to any legislative body in Tennessee.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Robby Mook becomes first openly gay major presidential campaign manager
description: Robby Mook took charge of Hillary Clinton's presidential campaign, the first openly gay person to manage a major US presidential bid.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US appoints its first Special Envoy for LGBT human rights
description: Randy Berry was appointed the first US Special Envoy for the Human Rights of LGBT Persons.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Obama names lesbian, bisexual and transgender people in a State of the Union
description: President Barack Obama used the words lesbian, bisexual and transgender in a State of the Union address, the first US president to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly transgender appointee joins the White House staff
description: President Obama appointed Raffi Freedman-Gurspan as an Outreach and Recruitment Director in the Presidential Personnel Office, the first openly transgender person appointed to work inside the White House.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cambodia's first LGBT magazine launches
description: Q Cambodia began publication as the country's first magazine for LGBT readers.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jamaica holds its first Pride celebrations
description: Jamaica staged its first LGBT Pride celebrations.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Zoey Tur becomes first openly trans reporter on US national TV
description: Zoey Tur joined Inside Edition as a special correspondent in February, the first openly transgender television reporter on national TV in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Thomas Roberts anchors network evening news as an openly gay man
description: Thomas Roberts anchored NBC Nightly News for a day, the first openly gay person to anchor an evening news broadcast on US network television.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex wedding vows broadcast on cable television
description: Lance Bass and Michael Turchin exchanged vows on air, the first same-sex couple to do so on US cable television.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The CW gets its first openly bisexual lead character
description: Screenwriter Jason Rothenberg confirmed that Clarke Griffin, the lead of The 100 played by Eliza Taylor, is bisexual — the first openly bisexual lead character on the CW network.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gay groups march in US St. Patrick's Day parades for the first time
description: Boston Pride and OutVets marched in Boston's St. Patrick's Day parade, OUT@NBCUniversal marched in New York City's, and The D.C. Center for the LGBT Community marched in Washington's — the first openly gay contingents admitted to each parade.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Boy Scouts of America lifts ban on openly gay adult leaders
description: The National Executive Board of the Boy Scouts ratified a resolution ending the national restriction on openly gay adult leaders and employees. Pascal Tessier, hired as a summer camp leader by the Greater New York Councils, became the first openly gay adult Boy Scout employed in the organization.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Patricia Velásquez comes out in her memoir Straight Walk
description: Model Patricia Velásquez published her memoir Straight Walk in February 2015, describing her upbringing in poverty in Venezuela and coming out as a lesbian — making her the first openly lesbian Latina supermodel.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly gay Latino pastor ordained in the ELCA
description: Rev. Ángel David Marrero was ordained in the Evangelical Lutheran Church in America in April 2015, the first openly gay Caribbean Latino pastor in the denomination.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andreja Pejic becomes the first openly trans model profiled by Vogue
description: Vogue profiled Andreja Pejic in its May 2015 issue, the first openly transgender model featured that way by the magazine.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Laverne Cox marks several firsts for openly trans women in US media
description: Laverne Cox posed for Allure's annual nudes issue as the first openly transgender actress to do so, received the first Madame Tussauds wax figure of an openly transgender person, and won a Daytime Emmy as executive producer of Laverne Cox Presents: The T Word — the first openly transgender woman to win in that role and the first trans documentary so honored.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Caitlyn Jenner appears on the cover of Vanity Fair
description: Caitlyn Jenner became the first openly transgender woman featured on the cover of Vanity Fair.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Fun Home opens on Broadway with a lesbian protagonist
description: Fun Home premiered on Broadway, the first Broadway musical centered on a lesbian protagonist.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
